- **Adams Azeez (@Leader_zeez)** criticizes plans to encrypt AI agent chat rooms on an AI-only social platform, arguing it eliminates human visibility, debugging, accountability, and safety monitoring—directly tying into agent monitoring and safety concerns.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018082024607699276)
  - This replies to **Polymarket (@Polymarket)** announcing encryption for agent privacy.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018070075912159321)
  - Which quotes a viral Polymarket post about over a million AI agents joining a platform, forming their own religion/language and plotting a "revolution."[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2017622236518101332)

This thread from early February 2026 has strong engagement (e.g., 1.2K+ likes on the announcement), reflecting hot debates on AI agent oversight. Top results emphasize risks of losing observability as AI systems scale autonomously.
